fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 74. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 70.

course_description.character: Genuinely part of ASO's official L'Étape by Tour de France series, produced by Kerry-based Elite Events Management and staged from Carrick-on-Suir — Sean Kelly and Sam Bennett's hometown, and the branding leans on that heritage. The 2025 edition (the most recent with published figures) offered two routes from Carrick-on-Suir: a 150 km / 1,932 m long route and a 94 km / 1,314 m short route marketed loosely as '100K' despite being 6 km shorter than that. Roughly 80% of the long course ran on closed roads through South Tipperary and Waterford's rolling hill country. The 2026 edition is confirmed in principle for the same town, but as of this write-up ASO's own calendar still lists the date as TBD.
course_description.signature_challenge: The timed KOM/QOM pair of Ticincor Hill (4.9 km at 7.9%, roughly 19 km into the route) and Seskin Hill (1.5 km at 7.9%, roughly 140 km in) — official timed climb classifications with their own jerseys, bookending most of the long course rather than arriving back-to-back.
suffering_zones: Ticincor Hill: a sustained 4.9 km climb averaging 7.9%, the longer of the two timed KOM/QOM segments, arriving early in the route.
Seskin Hill: shorter at 1.5 km but holding the same 7.9% average — arrives roughly 120 km later, with the day's fatigue already banked.
The timed sprint near Lemybrien, followed by the closing kilometres back into Carrick-on-Suir after Seskin has already taken its toll.

biased_opinion.verdict: Officially Licensed, Still Finding Its Footing
biased_opinion.summary: L'Étape Ireland is an entry in ASO's official L'Étape by Tour de France series, currently staged in Carrick-on-Suir, the hometown of Sean Kelly and Sam Bennett. The 2025 edition — its second, after debuting in Killarney in 2024 — drew roughly 1,400 starters on two routes (150 km / 1,932 m long, 94 km / 1,314 m short) with timed KOM/QOM classifications at Ticincor Hill and Seskin Hill, roughly 120 km apart. At least one published participant account praised the route, marshalling and feed support, but the evidence base for a broader positive consensus is thin. What's better documented: this is a young event that moved host towns after one year, entry pricing and Carrick-on-Suir accommodation both drew specific complaints in 2025, the official site's own published elevation figures for the long route are inconsistent across pages, and as of this write-up ASO's own calendar confirms the 2026 host town but not a date.
strengths: Genuinely official ASO L'Étape series event, appearing on ASO's own 2026 calendar
Real Irish cycling heritage: staged from Carrick-on-Suir, hometown of Sean Kelly and Sam Bennett
Two officially timed KOM/QOM climb classifications (Ticincor, Seskin) give the long route genuine competitive stakes beyond a supported ride
At least one detailed 2025 participant account praised route quality, marshalling, and feed-station support
weaknesses: Only two editions held to date — Killarney in 2024, Carrick-on-Suir in 2025 — including a host-town change after year one, with no 2026 date yet published
Specific 2025 rider/commenter complaints about entry pricing and scarce, expensive Carrick-on-Suir accommodation during event week
Official elevation figures for the long route are inconsistent across ASO's own published pages (figures ranging roughly 1,700-1,930 m have appeared) — treat any single number as approximate
Published registration and start counts suggest a substantial gap between sign-ups (nearly 2,000) and actual starters (~1,400) in 2025
2026 date remains unpublished on ASO's official calendar as of this write-up; the event's own homepage still displays stale 2025 registration messaging
biased_opinion.bottom_line: A legitimately licensed ASO event with real Irish cycling heritage and two officially timed climbs — worth watching, but it's a two-edition-old event that already changed host towns once, so don't book non-refundable travel until the 2026 date, routes, and registration are actually published.

final_verdict.one_liner: Officially licensed ASO event in Sean Kelly's hometown — real timed climbs, still-unsettled logistics.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want an officially licensed L'Étape event with real Irish cycling heritage and two closed-road timed climbs. Wait for the 2026 date, routes, and registration to be officially published before booking travel, and budget for scarce, pricier Carrick-on-Suir accommodation.
final_verdict.alternatives: For another officially licensed L'Étape event with a longer track record, look at L'Étape France or L'Étape Wales. For a similarly scaled Irish road sportive, see the Škoda Ring of Clare Sportive (also in this database).

videos.title: L'Etape Ireland 2025 Complete Experience: Racing Through Sean Kelly's Legendary Roads
videos.description:
Experience L'Etape Ireland 2025 from the inside! Join me as I tackle this incredible cycling sportive in Carrick on Suir, the legendary hometown of Irish cycling icons Sam Bennett and Sean Kelly. In this vlog-style video, I'll take you through the entire event experience - from the nervous start line energy to the challenging climbs and breathtaking Irish countryside. Whether you're considering entering L'Etape Ireland yourself or just love cycling events and sportives, this video gives you an authentic look at what makes this event so special. See the routes that shaped cycling legends, experience the atmosphere of a world-class sportive, and discover why Carrick on Suir is a must-visit destination for any cycling enthusiast.

Learn more and order 3D prints at - https://tinyurl.com/mtfa5xma

#LEtapeIreland #CyclingSportive #IrelandCycling #CarrickOnSuir #SamBennett #SeanKelly #CyclingVlog #RoadCycling #CyclingEvents #Sportive2025 #IrishCycling #CyclingIreland

eligibility.notes: ASO's official 2026 L'Étape series calendar (published 22 Jan 2026) lists 'L'Étape Ireland - Carrick-on-Suir' with date TBD; no 2026 route or registration page was found as of July 24, 2026 (the official homepage retains stale 2025 registration-open messaging). Confirmed as a genuine official ASO event (not merely Tour-themed), produced by Kerry-based Elite Events Management. Most recent confirmed edition: 2025 (Sept 28), second edition overall (debuted Killarney 2024), ~1,400 starters, 150km/1,932m long and 94km/1,314m short routes. Verified 2026-07-24 via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research, editorial wave 4.
